message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): response body bytes pass through verbatim (tick 371)

Added `distributed_http_client_response_body_bytes_pass_through_verbatim`
— pins that the client returns response body bytes exactly as
received, without CRLF normalization, null byte stripping, or UTF-8
transcoding.

Why this matters: distributed RPC bodies are opaque from the client's
perspective. Real payloads include:

- Serialized binary data (bincode, msgpack, protobuf) — all byte
  values 0-255 occur naturally.
- JSON payloads where string fields legitimately contain `\r\n`
  sequences (unescaped multi-line strings).
- Captured pane scrollback with mixed LF / CRLF line endings.

A regression that normalized body bytes (e.g. stripped trailing CR
before LF) would silently corrupt any of these payloads. The client
has no business interpreting body semantics.

Test body deliberately contains: CR, LF, CRLF, null byte (0x00),
high-bit byte (0xFF), tab, interleaved with ASCII. Server writes the
bytes verbatim after a Content-Length-bounded header. Client reads,
must return the identical byte sequence.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 2m 05s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
